docs.title: What are we doing here?
docs.text:
Our goal is to provide the Greek academic community (students for now) with a Linux distribution that will be tailored to their needs. That's why we made UniOS a free and open source Kubuntu respin and many apps to go along with it.

In those docs you will find the technical explanation for our project; we will explain the architecture and the building pipeline of the OS and of the apps that go along with it. If you want something a bit more light-hearted that could possibly go on a press release or to explain to a non-technical friend or colleague, you can visit our webpage.

docs.title: Current list of projects
docs.text:
As of now we have developed the following projects:

  • UniOS – It should be noted that this is the initial building script and it is of not much use after the first build.
  • UniBackpack – Our downloader for the software used by the departments of the Greek universities to teach each subject.
  • UniDesk – Our welcomer and helper app that runs on startup to greet the user and make them engage with our community.
  • UniMate – Our web app that serves as a bulk downloader for general use Linux apps.
  • UniNews – Our app that acts as a news aggregator for university news.
  • Our Webpage – This is our landing page that does a less technical introduction for our ecosystem.
  • The webpage that you are currently on – This is our documentation page that should be considered part of the workflow if you want to write for us, which we dearly suggest.

docs.title: Current list of packages available
docs.text:
The package that is currently maintained on our PPA is:

  • unios-desktop-settings
  • python3-unidesk
  • unibackpack

docs.title: Current list of PPA's
docs.text:
Our PPA that you can add on your (Debian/Ubuntu-based) system right now and get the UniOS experience is:

  • unios-core-stable

docs.title: General Workflow for developing UniOS or UniOS apps
docs.text: This document explains the general workflow of how to develop for UniOS and the apps that support it. Generally we will touch the subject of developing the apps first because UniOS currently undergoes general development.

docs.title: UniOS Apps
docs.text:
In general all development and especially apps are developed via issues, the following diagram explains the workflow visually:

Namely the steps can be reduced to:

  • Issue is being created by the UniOS team or the individual

  • An individual or a team member creates a new branch and tries to solve the issue

  • After submitting the pull request and after the reviews and adjustments the PR is accepted

  • If the change is significant (marked by the significant tag in the issue page) the change should be documented in UniDocs and pushed through a PR.

  • If the change is bumping a version number, then a release should be created and a changelog should be generated.

docs.title: UniOS
docs.text:
UniOS is developed a bit differently, because packages are a bit more weird you do need to have access to the PPA team, which for now only two people have for safety reasons.

Generally the package development cycle is the same with the apps development cycle, but when the PR passes and the build is completed the maintainers of the PPA will use dput to add the .source package to Canonical's servers.

docs.title: UniBackpack
docs.text: UniBackpack is a lightweight C++ Qt application designed to automate the installation of essential software and development tools for university students. By selecting the target University and Department, the application automatically detects the underlying Linux distribution's package manager (apt or pacman) and installs the required toolchain.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before building UniBackpack, ensure that the following dependencies are installed on your system:

  • A C++17 compatible compiler (gcc or clang)
  • CMake (version 3.10 or higher)
  • Qt6 Development Libraries
  • Polkit (pkexec) for granting installation privileges

docs.title: Installing Dependencies
docs.text:
For Arch Linux / Manjaro / EndeavourOS:

sudo pacman -S base-devel cmake qt6-base qt6-tools polkit

UniOS Logo For Debian / Ubuntu / Linux Mint:

sudo apt install build-essential cmake qt6-base-dev qt6-tools-dev-tools policykit-1

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
• src/ - C++ source files (.cpp)
  • include/ - C++ header files (.hpp)
  • ui/ - Qt Designer forms (.ui)
  • resources/lists/ - Configuration files containing the software lists tailored for each academic department.
  • resources/icons/ - Icons for each academic department.

docs.title: UniDesk
docs.text: UniDesk is the welcome application that ships with UniOS, a custom Linux distribution built for students and staff for all greek universities. It gives users a friendly starting point when they boot into the system, with quick access to information about the project, useful links, and the people behind it.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: When you open UniDesk you land on a simple screen with a two column layout. On the left you have introductory pages like Features, Links, and FAQ. On the right you have community oriented pages like Source Code, Contribute, and Credits. Clicking any button takes you to a dedicated page, and a back button always brings you home.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
Python 3.10 or newer and PyQt6.

pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Running it
docs.text: python src/unidesk/main.py

docs.title: Project structure
docs.text:
src/unidesk/
    main.py       entry point
    home.py       main window and all navigation logic
    pages.py      text content for each informational page
    credits.py    list of contributors
    links.py      external links shown in the Links page

To update any page content just open pages.py and edit the body text for that page. To add a new contributor open credits.py.

docs.title: 1. Introduction
docs.text:
UniNews is a desktop application designed to aggregate news, announcements, and updates from universities into a single interface.

University information is often distributed across multiple sources, including RSS feeds, departmental websites, faculty announcements, and institution-wide news portals. As a result, students, researchers, and staff must frequently navigate several websites to remain informed.

UniNews addresses this problem by collecting content from multiple sources and presenting it through a unified desktop application.

The application is developed in Python using PyQt6 and follows a modular architecture that supports both RSS feeds and custom website scrapers.

docs.title: 2. Objectives
docs.text:
The primary objectives of UniNews are:

  • Centralize university-related news.
  • Reduce the effort required to monitor multiple websites.
  • Provide a clean and modern desktop experience.
  • Support extensibility through community-contributed scrapers.
  • Enable offline access to previously retrieved articles.
  • Provide configurable notifications for new content.

docs.title: 3. System Architecture
docs.text: The system consists of five major components:

docs.title: User Interface Layer
docs.text:
Responsible for:

  • Displaying articles
  • Searching articles
  • Filtering content
  • Managing settings
  • Presenting notifications

Implemented using:

  • PyQt6

docs.title: Data Acquisition Layer
docs.text:
Responsible for collecting news from external sources.

Supports:

  • RSS feeds
  • Custom HTML scrapers

Implemented using:

  • feedparser
  • requests
  • BeautifulSoup

docs.title: Scraper Framework
docs.text:
Provides a standardized mechanism for integrating new content sources.

Each scraper inherits from a common base class and returns articles in a unified format.

docs.title: Persistence Layer
docs.text:
Responsible for:

  • Storing articles
  • Storing settings
  • Preventing duplicate entries

Implemented using:

  • SQLite

docs.title: Notification Layer
docs.text:
Responsible for desktop notifications whenever new articles are discovered.

Implemented using:

  • Plyer

docs.title: 4. Article Data Model
docs.text:
All sources are normalized into a common structure.

{
    "university": str,
    "source": str,
    "title": str,
    "summary": str,
    "link": str,
    "published": str,
    "fetched_at": str,
    "image_url": str
}

This allows RSS feeds and HTML scrapers to be handled identically by the application.

docs.title: 5. RSS Feed Support
docs.text:
RSS feeds are defined in:

data/feeds.json

Example:

{
  "name": "MIT News",
  "type": "rss",
  "url": "https://news.mit.edu/rss/feed"
}

The RSS service retrieves entries and converts them into the internal article format.

docs.title: 6. Scraper Framework
docs.text:
Scrapers are located inside:

scrapers/

Each scraper extends the abstract BaseScraper class.

Example:

class ExampleScraper(BaseScraper):

    def scrape(self):
        return []

Scrapers are registered through:

SCRAPER_REGISTRY

This design allows contributors to add support for new universities without modifying the rest of the system.

docs.title: 7. University of Macedonia Integration
docs.text:
The initial scraper implementation targets the University of Macedonia.

Supported sources include:

  • Main university news
  • Departmental announcements
  • Faculty-specific news pages

The scraper supports multiple subpages and categorizes articles by source.

Examples:

  • UoM Main News
  • UoM Applied Informatics
  • UoM Economics
  • UoM Business Administration

This categorization enables filtering directly from the application interface.

docs.title: 8. Local Storage
docs.text:
UniNews stores information locally within:

local_data/

Contents include:

docs.title: SQLite Database
docs.text:
Stores:

  • Articles
  • Sources
  • Metadata

docs.title: Settings File
docs.text:
Stores:

  • Theme preferences
  • Refresh intervals
  • Notification preferences
  • Cache limits

docs.title: 9. Search and Filtering
docs.text: The application provides:

docs.title: Global Search
docs.text:
Allows users to search:

  • Article titles
  • Article summaries
  • Source names

docs.title: Source Filtering
docs.text:
Users may filter content by:

  • University
  • Department
  • Individual source

This functionality is exposed through the sidebar navigation panel.

docs.title: 10. Notifications
docs.text:
The notification subsystem supports:

  • Desktop notifications
  • Keyword-based filtering
  • Optional startup refreshes
  • Periodic automatic refreshes

Users may define keywords that trigger notifications when matched within article content.

docs.title: 11. User Interface
docs.text:
The application follows a card-based design.

Key interface components include:

docs.title: Header
docs.text:
Displays:

  • Application title
  • Refresh controls

docs.title: Sidebar
docs.text:
Provides:

  • University filtering
  • Department filtering
  • Search functionality

docs.title: Article View
docs.text:
Displays:

  • Article image
  • Title
  • Source
  • Publication date
  • Summary

docs.title: Settings Dialog
docs.text:
Allows users to configure:

  • Theme
  • Refresh interval
  • Notification preferences
  • Cache settings

docs.title: 12. Extensibility
docs.text:
UniNews has been designed with extensibility as a primary goal.

New universities can be integrated through:

docs.title: RSS Feed Integration
docs.text:
Adding a new entry to:

feeds.json

docs.title: Scraper Integration
docs.text:
Implementing:

BaseScraper

and registering it within:

SCRAPER_REGISTRY

No modifications to the user interface or database layer are required.

docs.title: 13. Future Work
docs.text:
Planned enhancements include:

  • Additional university integrations
  • Improved image caching
  • Pagination support
  • Plugin-based scraper loading
  • OPML feed import/export
  • Article bookmarking
  • Automatic update checks
  • Native Linux package distribution
  • Cross-platform installers

docs.title: 14. License
docs.text:
UniNews is released under the GNU General Public License v3.0 (GPL-3.0).

The project is maintained as an open-source initiative and welcomes community contributions.
